medical_history.chief_complaint: voice hoarseness and dysphagia, and a 6-month history of left-sided blurred vision
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: 3-year history of voice hoarseness and dysphagia, and a 6-month history of left-sided blurred vision. Development of multiple palpable nodular masses of the left hand beginning at 7 years of age, later accompanied by formation of subcutaneous blue, soft, nontender masses, diagnosed as hemangiomas on physical examination.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Maffucci syndrome diagnosed based on radiographs of the left hand showing skeletal phalangeal malformation with calcific nodules and multiple enchondromas. Chest CT demonstrated enchondromas of the bilateral ribs and scapulae. Technetium-99m methylene diphosphonate whole-body bone scintigraphy revealed foci of intense tracer uptake, involving multiple bones.

physical_examination.general_examination: Multiple palpable nodular masses of the left hand, subcutaneous blue, soft, nontender masses diagnosed as hemangiomas.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological examination revealed left-sided deficits in hearing and gag reflex, bilateral temporal hemianopia, and decreased visual acuity (left eye 20/30; right eye 20/50). Brain MRI demonstrated 2 heterogeneously enhancing lesions, 1 in the left jugular foramen and the other in the suprasellar region suggestive of a pituitary macroadenoma.

laboratory_examinations.findings: c.394C > T (R132C) mutation detected in both pituitary adenoma and chondrosarcoma

radiographic_examinations.findings: Calcific nodules and lytic lesions of the phalanges, characteristic of enchondromas
Enchondromas of the bilateral ribs and scapulae

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: Technetium-99m methylene diphosphonate whole-body bone scintigraphy
radiographic_examinations.findings: Foci of intense tracer uptake, involving the right humerus, right ulna, right femur, right fibula, right tibia, left humerus, left radius, left hand, left femur, left fibula, and left tibia
Two heterogeneously enhancing lesions, one in the left jugular foramen and the other in the suprasellar region suggestive of a pituitary macroadenoma

other_tests.findings: Left-sided deficits in hearing and gag reflex, bilateral temporal hemianopia, and decreased visual acuity (left eye 20/30; right eye 20/50)
Negative for GH, PRL, LH, FSH, TSH, and ACTH; positive for synaptophysin confirming neuroendocrine origin

Grade II chondrosarcoma characterized by moderate cellularity with occasional mitotic figures, cellular atypia, and increased muco-myxoid degeneration of chondroid matrix; pituitary adenoma demonstrated a highly cellular tumor with prominent monomorphism and loss of the normal reticulin meshwork

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Maffucci syndrome associated with a nonfunctional pituitary adenoma
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with multiple enchondromas and hemangiomas, characteristic of Maffucci syndrome. Brain MRI revealed two intracranial tumors: a jugular foramen chondrosarcoma and a suprasellar lesion suggestive of a pituitary macroadenoma. Histopathological examination confirmed the diagnoses of chondrosarcoma and pituitary adenoma. DNA sequencing demonstrated identical IDH1 mutations in both tumors, supporting the inclusion of pituitary adenomas among tumors characterizing Maffucci syndrome.
